stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Engineers have developed a thermal-storage brick that can retain about 88% of its heat after ten days.
  • The brick is made from a paraffin-based wax (a substance that melts when heated and hardens at cool temperatures).
  • When the wax is in a liquid state, heat flows into and out of the brick.
  • When the wax is in a solid state, heat stops flowing and remains locked inside the brick.
  • The stored heat can be released later by warming the brick above the wax’s melting point.
stem: The student wants to specify how the wax enables heat storage. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Engineers have developed a thermal-storage brick that contains paraffin-based wax, which melts when heated and hardens at cool temperatures.
When the wax in a thermal-storage brick hardens at cool temperatures, heat stops flowing and is locked inside the brick.
The stored heat in a thermal-storage brick, which contains paraffin-based wax, can be released by warming the brick.
Paraffin-based wax allows a thermal-storage brick to retain about 88% of its heat after ten days.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence specifies how the wax in a thermal-storage brick enables heat storage, explaining that heat stops flowing and is locked inside the brick when the wax hardens at cool temperatures.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes some properties of the wax; it doesn’t specify how the wax enables heat storage. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence indicates how stored heat can be released; it doesn’t specify how the wax enables storage. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence specifies how much heat the brick retains; it doesn’t specify how the wax enables storage.
